page: Microsoft Azure D64ads_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DADSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 32 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Local storage is provided via a 2458 GB disk, and the instance supports Premium Storage. No dedicated GPU hardware is present on this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression all placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Memory subsystem performance is highly polarized: the PassMark memory score ranks in the top 10% and cached read-only memory bandwidth is in the top 25%, whereas uncached large-block read and write bandwidths fall into the middle 50%. Single-core execution, including Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression, also performs at an average level.

Given its balanced vCPU-to-memory ratio and substantial 2458 GB local storage, this instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for resource-dense workloads that do not require GPU acceleration. It is highly capable for multi-threaded tasks such as software compilation, database operations, and Redis deployments, where it achieves 63.62 million SET operations per second. It is also suitable for medium-scale LLM text generation, though large-scale model inference is constrained by the lack of dedicated accelerators and average uncached memory bandwidth.
description: Microsoft Azure D64ads_v5 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine powered by a 32-core AMD EPYC 7763 processor, delivering 64 dedicated vCPUs at 3.525 GHz. It features 256.0 GB of RAM and 2458 GB of local storage, with support for Premium Storage. Benchmarks show strong multi-core performance, placing in the top 25% for multi-threaded decompression, software compilation, and database operations, while its memory score ranks in the top 10%. However, single-core performance and uncached memory bandwidth remain average. Lacking a GPU, this instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for high-density general-purpose workloads, multi-threaded applications, Redis key-value stores, and database hosting.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D64ads_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, 256 GB of RAM, and 2458 GB of local storage, delivering strong multi-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D64ads_v5 features 64 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2458 GB local storage for balanced multi-core workloads.
tagline: A balanced multi-core virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD processors, substantial local storage, and high-density memory for general-purpose enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory paired with 2458 GB of local storage
Top 10% performance in PassMark memory benchmarks
Strong multi-core capabilities for software compilation and database operations
Suited for Redis deployments, database hosting, and multi-threaded workloads
